fixed.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist: FinTech Application

## Scope: Transactions up to $1,000,000

**Document Version:** 1.0 | **Classification:** Confidential | **Last Updated:** January 2025

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This comprehensive security audit checklist establishes mandatory controls for a FinTech application processing high-value transactions up to **$1,000,000 per transaction**. The scope addresses four critical security domains: OWASP Top 10 application security, PCI-DSS v4.0 compliance, fraud detection mechanisms, and multi-jurisdictional regulatory requirements.

**Critical Findings Summary:**

| Domain | Risk Level | Key Gap |
|--------|------------|---------|
| OWASP A01-A10 Coverage | HIGH | Incomplete threat modeling; missing A04, A06-A10 |
| PCI-DSS v4.0 Alignment | HIGH | No key management controls; penetration testing methodology absent |
| Fraud Detection | CRITICAL | Real-time monitoring thresholds undefined; velocity checks missing |
| Regulatory Compliance | MEDIUM | Multi-jurisdictional requirements not fully mapped |

**Risk Quantification:** With an average financial services data breach costing **$4.2 million** (IBM, 2023), and **91%** of financial organizations experiencing fraud attempts in 2022, this application presents a high-value target requiring defense-in-depth controls across all identified gaps.

**Regulatory Exposure:** PCI-DSS v4.0 enforcement began March 2024 with 64 new requirements. Non-compliance risks penalties of **$5,000 to $100,000 per month** and potential transaction processing suspension.

**Recommended Priority Actions:**

1. Implement dual-authorization for transactions exceeding $50,000
2. Establish real-time fraud detection with velocity and behavioral checks
3. Complete OWASP Top 10 coverage, particularly A04 (Insecure Design)
4. Deploy Hardware Security Module (HSM) for encryption key management
5. Conduct penetration testing aligned with PCI-DSS Requirement 11.3

---

## 2. Key Findings

### 2.1 Statistical Context and Risk Baseline

| Finding | Data Point | Source | Implication |
|---------|------------|--------|-------------|
| Fraud Prevalence | 91% of financial organizations experienced fraud attempts (2022) | AFP Payments Fraud Survey, 2023 | Universal threat requiring proactive controls |
| API Vulnerability | 67% of financial APIs contain vulnerabilities allowing account takeover | Salt Security State of API Security, 2022 | API security is critical attack vector |
| Breach Cost | $4.2M average cost of financial services data breach | IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report, 2023 | Risk quantification for control investment |
| Regulatory Evolution | PCI-DSS v4.0 introduced 64 new requirements | PCI-SSC, 2022 | Compliance landscape has shifted significantly |
| Authentication Mandate | MFA required for financial access >$10K per NIST SP 800-63B | NIST, 2017 | Baseline authentication standard |

### 2.2 Control Domain Assessment

#### 2.2.1 Access Control (A01:2021)

**Current Status:** PARTIAL COMPLIANCE

| Requirement | Testing Method | Frequency | Pass Criteria | Status |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|---------------|--------|
| Direct object reference protection | Automated + manual IDOR testing | Each release | No unauthorized data exposure via enumeration | ☑ Required |
| Function-level access control | Role matrix testing across endpoints | Quarterly | 100% coverage of access matrices | ☑ Required |
| Multi-tenant data isolation | Cross-tenant access attempt testing | Monthly | Zero cross-tenant data leakage | ☑ Required |
| Rate limiting on privileged operations | Load testing with authenticated sessions | Weekly | >200 req/min triggers block | ☑ Required |
| API access control validation | OAuth2/SAML token inspection | Each release | Token scopes match endpoint permissions | ☑ Required |
| CORS policy enforcement | Configuration review | Each deployment | Whitelist-only origins | ⚠ Gap |
| Server-side session validation | Request interception testing | Each release | No session fixation possible | ⚠ Gap |

**$1M Transaction-Specific Controls:**

| Control | Implementation Requirement |
|---------|---------------------------|
| Dual Authorization | Transactions >$50,000 require secondary approval |
| Approval Timeout | Both authorizers must authenticate within 15-minute window |
| Out-of-Band Verification | High-value transactions (> $250,000) require phone confirmation |
| Audit Trail | Immutable logging of approval chain with timestamps and IP addresses |
| Segregation of Duties | Initiator cannot approve own transaction |

#### 2.2.2 Cryptographic Failures (A02:2021)

**Current Status:** INCOMPLETE COMPLIANCE

```
REQUIREMENT                                   COMPLIANCE STATUS
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TLS 1.3 enforced at load balancer            ☐ Required
TLS 1.0/1.1 disabled                         ☐ Required
Certificate pinning (mobile apps)            ☐ 3-year rotation minimum
AES-256 encryption for PII at rest           ☐ Required
HSM for key management                       ☐ PCI-DSS Req 3.6
No MD5/SHA-1 for password hashing           ☐ Argon2id required
Cardholder data truncation post-auth         ☐ PAN never stored post-authorization
Key rotation schedule                        ☐ Annual minimum
Encryption key access logging                ☐ Aligned withReq 10.1
```

#### 2.2.3 Injection (A03:2021)

**Current Status:** HIGH PRIORITY GAP

| Vulnerability Category | Testing Method | Controls Required |
|-----------------------|----------------|-------------------|
| SQL Injection | Static and dynamic analysis, parameterized queries review | Input validation, prepared statements, ORM usage |
| NoSQL Injection | Document store security testing | Input sanitization, query parameterization |
| LDAP Injection | Context-aware encoding | LDAP DN encoding, input validation |
| OS Command Injection | Source code analysis, fuzzing | Avoid system calls with user input; use API libraries |
| XPath Injection | XML document security testing | Parameterized XPath queries, input validation |
| Expression Language Injection | Template engine review | Sandboxed evaluation contexts |

**Transaction-Specific Injection Controls:**

- All transaction parameters must undergo type, range, and format validation
- Transaction amount fields must reject negative values, overflow, and precision errors
- Reference IDs must be validated against existing transaction records
- Web application firewall (WAF) rules must block common injection patterns

#### 2.2.4 Insecure Design (A04:2021)

**Current Status:** CRITICAL GAP IDENTIFIED

Threat modeling for financial transaction processing must address:

| Threat Modeling Component | Required Deliverable |
|---------------------------|---------------------|
| Asset Inventory | Transaction data flows, PII stores, cryptographic assets |
| Data Flow Diagram | All pathways from user input to transaction processing backend |
| Trust Boundary Mapping | Internal vs. external service boundaries |
| Attack Surface Analysis | API endpoints, mobile APIs, web interfaces, batch processing |
| Abuse Case Documentation | Transaction manipulation, fraud scenarios, data exfiltration |
| Countermeasure Mapping | Controls mapped to identified threats |

**Secure Design Requirements:**

- [ ] STRIDE threat modeling completed for all transaction flows
- [ ] Secure design review gate in SDLC prior to development
- [ ] Architecture review board approval for payment processing changes
- [ ] Secure coding standards adopted and enforced via CI/CD pipeline scanning
- [ ] Component dependency vulnerability scanning (weekly minimum)

#### 2.2.5 Security Misconfiguration (A05:2021)

**Current Status:** REMEDIATION REQUIRED

| Configuration Area | Requirement | Verification Method |
|-------------------|-------------|---------------------|
| Server hardening | CIS benchmarks for all transaction servers | Automated configuration scanner |
| Database configuration | No default credentials; principle of least privilege | Credential audit |
| Application framework | Unnecessary features disabled; debug mode off in production | Deployment checklist |
| Error handling | Custom error pages; no stack traces exposed | Penetration testing |
| Cloud security | S3 buckets private; IAM roles follow least privilege | Cloud security posture review |
| API security | API gateway rate limiting; documented API versions | API inventory and testing |

#### 2.2.6 Vulnerable Components (A06:2021)

**Current Status:** MONITORING REQUIRED

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Software inventory | Complete Bill of Materials (SBOM) for all transaction processing components |
| Vulnerability monitoring | CVE database monitoring; alerts for financial libraries |
| Patch management | Critical patches within 72 hours; regular maintenance windows |
| Dependency scanning | Automated scanning in CI/CD; block builds with critical vulnerabilities |
| License compliance | Legal review of all open-source components in transaction flow |

#### 2.2.7 Authentication Failures (A07:2021)

**Current Status:** PARTIAL COMPLIANCE

NIST SP 800-63B mandates MFA for financial access exceeding $10,000. For $1M transactions:

| Control | Requirement |
|---------|-------------|
| Multi-Factor Authentication | Mandatory for all transaction initiation |
| Adaptive Authentication | Step-up authentication for anomalies (new device, location, time) |
| Password Requirements | Minimum 12 characters; no common passwords; breach detection |
| Session Management | Secure session tokens; automatic timeout after 15 minutes inactivity |
| Credential Storage | Argon2id hashing; no plaintext passwords in logs or databases |
| Brute Force Protection | ≤5 failed attempts triggers 15-minute lockout |

#### 2.2.8 Software and Data Integrity Failures (A08:2021)

**Current Status:** INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIRED

| Control | Implementation |
|---------|----------------|
| Code signing | All transaction processing code signed; verified at deployment |
| CI/CD security | No unsigned artifacts in pipeline; secrets management via vault |
| Dependency integrity | Package manager integrity checks; locked dependency versions |
| Deployment verification | Hash verification of deployed artifacts |
| Configuration integrity | Signed configuration files; drift detection |

#### 2.2.9 Logging and Monitoring Failures (A09:2021)

**Current Status:** CRITICAL GAPS IDENTIFIED

| Event Type | Logged Data | Retention |
|-----------|-------------|-----------|
| Authentication attempts | User ID, timestamp, IP, success/failure, MFA method | 1 year |
| Transaction initiation | User, amount, recipient, timestamp, session ID, device fingerprint | 5 years |
| Authorization decisions | Approver, timestamp, method (in-band/out-of-band) | 5 years |
| Administrative actions | Admin ID, action, timestamp, affected records | 5 years |
| System errors | Error type, stack trace (sanitized), timestamp, correlation ID | 1 year |
| Security events | WAF blocks, rate limit triggers, anomaly detections | 1 year |

**Monitoring Requirements:**

- [ ] Real-time alerting for >3 failed auth attempts within 5 minutes
- [ ] Alert escalation for transaction anomalies (velocity, amount deviation)
- [ ] SIEM integration with 1-hour maximum log ingestion delay
- [ ] Alert correlation for compound attack detection
- [ ] Quarterly log review by security operations

#### 2.2.10 Server-Side Request Forgery (A10:2021)

**Current Status:** MITIGATION REQUIRED

| Control | Implementation |
|---------|----------------|
| URL validation | Block internal IP ranges, localhost, cloud metadata endpoints |
| Allowlist enforcement | Only pre-approved external service URLs |
| Network segmentation | Transaction processing isolated from general workload |
| URL parsing | Use built-in URL parsing libraries; never manual string concatenation |

---

## 3. PCI-DSS v4.0 Compliance Framework

PCI-DSS v4.0 introduced 64 new requirements with enforcement beginning March 2024. The following checklist addresses all 12 requirements with financial transaction applicability.

### 3.1 Requirement 1: Firewall Configuration

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Network diagram | Current diagram documenting all cardholder data flows |
| Firewall rules | Documented rules for all traffic flows to/from CDE |
| Inbound/outbound rules | Restrictive defaults; only explicitly permitted traffic |
| DMZ configuration | Public-facing services isolated from CDE |
| Personal device policy | No personal devices accessing CDE |
| Wireless network controls | Wireless networks segmented; WPA3 or WPA2-Enterprise |

### 3.2 Requirement 2: Vendor Default Accounts

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Default password changes | All vendor default passwords changed before production |
| Unnecessary accounts | Unnecessary accounts removed or disabled |
| Security baselines | Secure configuration standards applied to all systems |

### 3.3 Requirement 3: Stored Cardholder Data

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| PAN protection | Cardholder data encrypted at rest; PAN never stored post-authorization |
| Cryptographic key management | Keys stored in HSM; key custodian rotation |
| Key procedures | Documented key management procedures; split knowledge |
| Key rotation | Annual rotation or per transaction if risk warrants |
| Key storage | Encryption keys separate from encrypted data |
| Strong cryptography | AES-256 or equivalent; no deprecated algorithms |

**Truncation vs. Tokenization:**

- Transactions may store tokenized PANs for idempotency
- Original PAN stored only during authorization window (< 24 hours)
- Token-to-PAN mapping stored separately with enhanced access controls

### 3.4 Requirement 4: Data Transmission

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| TLS 1.2+ required | All PAN transmission must use TLS 1.2 or higher |
| Certificate validation | Proper certificate chain validation; no trust of self-signed certs |
| Endpoint verification | TLS pinning for mobile applications |
| Key exchange | Only trusted certificates in trust store; expiration monitoring |

### 3.5 Requirement 5: Vulnerability Management

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| AV/Anti-malware | Current antivirus deployed on all systems |
| Vulnerability scanning | Internal and external scans; quarterly by qualified scanner |
| Patch management | Critical patches within 30 days; emergency patches ASAP |
| Change detection | File integrity monitoring on CDE |

### 3.6 Requirement 6: Secure System Development

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| SDLC security | Security requirements in all development phases |
| Data purging | Cardholder data purged when no longer needed |
| Buffer overflow prevention | Developer training; secure coding standards |
| Change management | Documented changes; rollback procedures |
| Live production access | Separate development/test environments; no live PAN in test |

### 3.7 Requirement 7: Access Control

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Least privilege | Access limited to least privilege required |
| Role-based access | Documented roles mapped to system access |
| Physical access controls | Badge access; visitor logs; camera coverage |
| Multi-tenant isolation | Logical separation of customer data |
| CDE access restriction | Access limited to personnel with business need |

### 3.8 Requirement 8: Identity and Authentication

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Unique IDs | Individual user IDs for all access; no shared accounts |
| MFA for CDE access | Multi-factor authentication for all CDE access |
| MFA for remote access | MFA required for all remote access |
| Credential management | 90-day maximum credential lifetime; no reuse of last 4 |
| Lockout policy | Account lockout after 6 failed attempts |
| Session timeout | 15-minute inactivity timeout |
| User authentication verification | Current authentication required for password changes |

### 3.9 Requirement 9: Physical Security

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Data center controls | Badge access; visitor escort; mantraps |
| Media handling | Secure storage; physical media destruction |
| POS/device security | Tamper detection; regular inspection |

### 3.10 Requirement 10: Logging and Monitoring

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Audit trail | All access to cardholder data logged |
| Log protection | Logs tamper-proof; access restricted |
| Alerting | Automated alerting for anomalies |
| Log correlation | Centralized logging; SIEM integration |
| Retention | Minimum 1 year log retention; 3 years preferred |
| Daily log review | Automated process; exceptions investigated |

### 3.11 Requirement 11: Testing

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Network penetration testing | Annual by qualified tester; vulnerabilities remediated |
| Segmentation testing | Annual verification of CDE isolation |
| Vulnerability scanning | Quarterly internal; quarterly external; monthly after changes |
| IDS/IPS | Intrusion detection/prevention on CDE |
| Change detection | File integrity monitoring; alerts for unauthorized changes |

**Penetration Testing Methodology (Per Req 11.3):**

- [ ] Annual external penetration test by qualified assessor
- [ ] Annual internal penetration test
- [ ] Segmentation testing to verify CDE isolation
- [ ] Web application testing including transaction flows
- [ ] API security testing for payment APIs
- [ ] Social engineering testing (optional but recommended)
- [ ] Remediation tracking with retesting confirmation

### 3.12 Requirement 12: Information Security Program

| Control | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Security policy | Annual policy review; documented responsibilities |
| Risk assessment | Annual; addresses threats and vulnerabilities |
| Security awareness | Annual training; phishing simulation quarterly |
| Personnel screening | Background checks for personnel with CDE access |
| Incident response | Documented plan; annual testing |
| Vendor management | SLAs with security requirements; annual reviews |
| Documentation | All security procedures documented and current |

---

## 4. Fraud Detection Controls

### 4.1 Real-Time Transaction Monitoring

| Threshold Type | Threshold Value | Action |
|---------------|-----------------|--------|
| Single transaction amount | > $100,000 | Review queue; enhanced monitoring |
| Single transaction amount | > $500,000 | Manual review required before processing |
| Single transaction amount | > $750,000 | Dual authorization + compliance approval |
| Hourly velocity | > 3 transactions | Velocity alert; behavior analysis |
| Daily volume | > 10 transactions or $500,000 | Account review |
|

gnn.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist: FinTech Application Handling Transactions Up to $1M

**Document Classification:** Security Audit Framework  
**Applicable Standards:** OWASP Mobile Top 10 (2024), OWASP API Security Top 10 (2023), PCI-DSS v4.0, Regulatory Framework Requirements  
**Audit Scope:** FinTech applications processing transactions ≤$1,000,000 USD annually  
**Document Version:** 1.0  

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This security audit checklist provides a systematic framework for evaluating the security posture of FinTech applications that process transactions up to $1 million annually. The checklist synthesizes four critical security domains: OWASP compliance (mobile and API), PCI-DSS requirements, fraud detection mechanisms, and regulatory compliance obligations.

### Scope Coverage Matrix

| Security Domain | Standard/Framework | Application Scope | Risk Classification |
|-----------------|--------------------|-------------------|---------------------|
| Application Security | OWASP Mobile Top 10 (2024) | Mobile client, API layer | CRITICAL |
| Application Security | OWASP API Security Top 10 (2023) | Backend services, integrations | CRITICAL |
| Payment Security | PCI-DSS v4.0 | Merchant Level 2-4 (scoped) | CRITICAL |
| Fraud Detection | Custom + Industry Best Practices | Transaction processing | HIGH |
| Regulatory Compliance | GLBA, SOX, SOC 2, BSA/AML | Data handling, reporting | HIGH |

### Critical Risk Areas Requiring Immediate Attention

1. **Authentication and Session Management** — Improper implementation leads to unauthorized fund access
2. **Cryptographic Key Management** — Failure results in data exposure and regulatory violations
3. **Data Transmission Security** — Intercepted communications enable transaction manipulation
4. **Transaction Integrity Controls** — Fraud bypass compromises financial integrity
5. **KYC/AML Compliance** — Non-compliance risks regulatory sanctions and license revocation

### Compliance Thresholds

| Standard | Classification | Qualification Criteria |
|----------|---------------|-----------------------|
| PCI-DSS Merchant Level | Level 2 | 20,000 to 1,000,000 annual Visa transactions |
| PCI-DSS Merchant Level | Level 3 | 20,000 to 1,000,000 annual Visa e-commerce transactions |
| PCI-DSS Merchant Level | Level 4 | <20,000 annual Visa e-commerce transactions |
| SOC 2 Type II | Trust Services Criteria | All FinTech applications handling customer funds |

---

## SECTION 1: OWASP COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST

### 1.1 OWASP Mobile Top 10 (2024) — Critical Items

The OWASP Mobile Top 10 addresses the most prevalent security vulnerabilities in mobile applications. The following checklist applies to native (iOS/Android) and cross-platform FinTech applications.

#### M1: Improper Credential Usage (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M1.1 | Verify Keychain (iOS) or Keystore (Android) usage for all credential storage | CRITICAL | Code review showing Keychain/Keystore API calls |
| M1.2 | Confirm KeychainAccessControl flags set to `kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly` or equivalent | CRITICAL | Security configuration documentation |
| M1.3 | Validate no sensitive data stored in `NSUserDefaults`, `SharedPreferences`, or equivalent plain-text storage | CRITICAL | Data storage audit report |
| M1.4 | Verify all API keys, tokens, and secrets stored in secure enclave or hardware-backed keystore | CRITICAL | Penetration testing report |
| M1.5 | Confirm no hardcoded credentials, API keys, or symmetric keys in application binary | CRITICAL | Static analysis scan report (e.g., MobSF, SonarQube) |
| M1.6 | Review biometric authentication integration: `LAContext` (iOS) / `BiometricPrompt` (Android) policy flags | HIGH | Implementation documentation |
| M1.7 | Verify app does not request excessive device permissions beyond functional requirements | HIGH | Permission audit manifest |

**Testing Procedures for M1:**
```
□ Run automated secret scanning (GitLeaks, TruffleHog)
□ Attempt Frida instrumentation to extract Keychain contents
□ Decompile application binary and search for embedded secrets
□ Verify certificate/public key pinning does not rely on hardcoded certificates
```

#### M2: Inadequate Supply Chain Security (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M2.1 | Generate and maintain Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for all third-party dependencies | HIGH | SBOM document (SPDX or CycloneDX format) |
| M2.2 | Verify dependency vulnerability scanning implemented in CI/CD pipeline | HIGH | CI/CD configuration and scan results |
| M2.3 | Confirm all SDK integrations from FinTech service providers verified for security compliance | HIGH | SDK vendor security assessments |
| M2.4 | Validate container/image signing for backend microservices | MEDIUM | Image signing certificates and verification logs |
| M2.5 | Review third-party analytics/ads SDKs removed from production builds | HIGH | Build configuration review |

#### M3: Insecure Authentication/Authorization (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M3.1 | Verify OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect implementation follows RFC 6749/6750 specifications | CRITICAL | OAuth flow documentation and test results |
| M3.2 | Confirm JWT tokens use RS256 or ES256 signing algorithms (not HS256 with shared secrets) | CRITICAL | Token validation code review |
| M3.3 | Verify access tokens expire within 15-60 minutes and refresh token rotation implemented | CRITICAL | Token lifecycle testing |
| M3.4 | Validate authorization matrix enforces principle of least privilege | CRITICAL | Authorization test cases |
| M3.5 | Confirm multi-factor authentication (MFA) available for high-risk operations | HIGH | MFA implementation documentation |
| M3.6 | Verify step-up authentication for transactions exceeding defined thresholds | CRITICAL | Step-up authentication test results |
| M3.7 | Review authentication state management: secure session storage, logout propagation | CRITICAL | Session management test results |

**Authorization Matrix Template:**
```
┌────────────────────────────┬───────────┬───────────┬───────────┬───────────┐
│ Operation                  │ Level 0   │ Level 1   │ Level 2   │ Level 3   │
│                            │ Guest     │ Authenticated │ MFA Verified │ Admin │
├────────────────────────────┼───────────┼───────────┼───────────┼───────────┤
│ View Account Balance       │ ✗         │ ✓         │ ✓         │ ✓         │
│ View Transaction History   │ ✗         │ ✓         │ ✓         │ ✓         │
│ Initiate P2P Transfer      │ ✗         │ ✗         │ ✓         │ ✓         │
│ Add Beneficiary            │ ✗         │ ✗         │ ✓         │ ✓         │
│ Increase Limits            │ ✗         │ ✗         │ ✗         │ ✓         │
│ View Audit Logs            │ ✗         │ ✗         │ ✗         │ ✓         │
└────────────────────────────┴───────────┴───────────┴───────────┴───────────┘
```

#### M4: Insufficient Input/Output Validation (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M4.1 | Verify all user inputs sanitized before database storage | CRITICAL | Input validation test results |
| M4.2 | Confirm parameterize queries/prepared statements used for all database interactions | CRITICAL | Code review showing query patterns |
| M4.3 | Validate API request payload schemas with strict type enforcement | CRITICAL | Schema validation test results |
| M4.4 | Implement output encoding appropriate for rendering context (HTML, JSON, XML) | CRITICAL | Output encoding review |
| M4.5 | Test for injection vulnerabilities: SQL, NoSQL, LDAP, OS command injection | CRITICAL | Penetration testing report |
| M4.6 | Verify file upload validation includes content-type verification, size limits, malware scanning | HIGH | File upload test results |

#### M5: Insecure Communication (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M5.1 | Verify TLS 1.2 minimum (TLS 1.3 recommended) for all network communications | CRITICAL | TLS configuration audit |
| M5.2 | Confirm certificate pinning implemented on all API endpoints | CRITICAL | Certificate pinning test results |
| M5.3 | Validate no HTTP traffic except to explicitly whitelisted endpoints | CRITICAL | Network traffic analysis |
| M5.4 | Verify custom URL schemes (deep links) do not expose tokens or sensitive data | HIGH | Deep link security review |
| M5.5 | Confirm mTLS implemented for service-to-service communications | HIGH | mTLS configuration documentation |
| M5.6 | Verify WebView configurations disable JavaScript access to native APIs | HIGH | WebView security review |

#### M6: Inadequate Privacy Controls (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M6.1 | Create and maintain data classification map identifying PII, PHI, financial data | HIGH | Data classification document |
| M6.2 | Verify PII inventory documents all locations where personal data is stored | HIGH | PII inventory report |
| M6.3 | Confirm privacy policy accurately reflects data collection practices (GDPR Article 13, CCPA) | HIGH | Privacy policy review |
| M6.4 | Implement data minimization: collect only data necessary for specified purpose | HIGH | Data flow analysis |
| M6.5 | Verify consent management implemented for data collection beyond contractual necessity | HIGH | Consent management documentation |
| M6.6 | Confirm data retention policies enforced with automated purging | HIGH | Data retention configuration review |

#### M7: Insufficient Binary Protections (MEDIUM)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M7.1 | Verify root/jailbreak detection implemented (not relied upon as sole control) | MEDIUM | Root detection test results |
| M7.2 | Confirm code obfuscation applied to critical business logic | MEDIUM | Obfuscation configuration review |
| M7.3 | Implement anti-tampering: verify integrity checks on application binary | MEDIUM | Integrity verification test results |
| M7.4 | Verify debugger detection prevents runtime analysis | MEDIUM | Debugger detection test results |
| M7.5 | Confirm SSL pinning cannot be bypassed via hooking frameworks (Frida detection) | MEDIUM | Pinning bypass test results |

#### M8: Security Misconfiguration (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M8.1 | Verify default credentials changed for all backend services and third-party integrations | CRITICAL | Credential audit report |
| M8.2 | Confirm debug features disabled in production builds | HIGH | Build configuration review |
| M8.3 | Validate secure configuration headers: HSTS, CSP, X-Content-Type-Options | HIGH | HTTP security header test results |
| M8.4 | Verify cloud storage (S3, GCS) buckets not publicly accessible | CRITICAL | Cloud storage ACL review |
| M8.5 | Confirm unnecessary ports, services, and endpoints disabled | HIGH | Network port scan results |

#### M9: Insecure Data Storage (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M9.1 | Verify encrypted database using AES-256 for local data storage | CRITICAL | Encryption configuration review |
| M9.2 | Confirm encryption keys stored separately from encrypted data | CRITICAL | Key storage architecture review |
| M9.3 | Verify secure deletion: data overwritten before deletion from local storage | MEDIUM | Secure deletion implementation review |
| M9.4 | Validate no sensitive data logged to application logs or crash reports | HIGH | Log analysis review |
| M9.5 | Confirm backup encryption when device backups enabled | HIGH | Backup encryption test results |

#### M10: Insufficient Cryptography (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| M10.1 | Verify no use of deprecated algorithms: MD5, SHA-1 (for hashing), DES, 3DES, RC4 | CRITICAL | Algorithm inventory review |
| M10.2 | Confirm AES-256-CBC or AES-256-GCM used for symmetric encryption | CRITICAL | Encryption implementation review |
| M10.3 | Verify cryptographic random number generation using platform secure random APIs | CRITICAL | RNG implementation review |
| M10.4 | Validate key derivation functions (KDF) use PBKDF2 (≥100,000 iterations), Argon2, or scrypt | HIGH | KDF configuration review |
| M10.5 | Confirm no custom cryptographic implementations; use platform-native crypto libraries | CRITICAL | Crypto library audit |

---

### 1.2 OWASP API Security Top 10 (2023)

The OWASP API Security Top 10 addresses vulnerabilities specific to API endpoints, which FinTech applications heavily rely upon for transaction processing and data exchange.

#### API1: Broken Object Level Authorization (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API1.1 | Verify object-level access controls enforced server-side for all API endpoints | CRITICAL | Authorization test cases |
| API1.2 | Validate API responses do not expose unauthorized object data | CRITICAL | Response data validation test |
| API1.3 | Confirm indirect object reference vulnerabilities remediated | CRITICAL | IDOR test results |
| API1.4 | Implement and verify resource ownership verification for all tenant-scoped operations | CRITICAL | Multi-tenant isolation test |

**Testing Procedure for API1:**
```
□ Enumerate all API endpoints and identify object references
□ For each endpoint, test accessing objects belonging to other users
□ Verify 403 Forbidden returned for unauthorized access attempts
□ Confirm API responses filtered based on authorization context
```

#### API2: Broken Authentication (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API2.1 | Verify authentication mechanisms properly implemented per API security best practices | CRITICAL | Authentication implementation review |
| API2.2 | Confirm brute force protection: account lockout, progressive delays, CAPTCHA after failed attempts | CRITICAL | Brute force protection test |
| API2.3 | Validate weak authentication vulnerability assessment completed | CRITICAL | Authentication vulnerability scan |
| API2.4 | Verify credential stuffing protection implemented | HIGH | Credential stuffing test results |
| API2.5 | Confirm authentication token strength: sufficient entropy, proper length | CRITICAL | Token analysis |

#### API3: Broken Object Property Level Authorization (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API3.1 | Verify API responses only include properties the requesting user is authorized to view | HIGH | Response property audit |
| API3.2 | Validate write-up protection: users cannot modify properties they should not access | HIGH | Property modification test |
| API3.3 | Confirm field-level access control implemented for all sensitive data fields | HIGH | Field access control review |

#### API4: Unrestricted Resource Consumption (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API4.1 | Verify API rate limiting implemented and tested | CRITICAL | Rate limiting test results |
| API4.2 | Confirm response payload size limits enforced | HIGH | Payload size limit test |
| API4.3 | Validate execution timeouts for all API operations | HIGH | Timeout configuration review |
| API4.4 | Implement and verify resource quotas per client/tenant | HIGH | Quota enforcement test |
| API4.5 | Confirm memory consumption limits for file upload and processing | HIGH | Memory limit test |

#### API5: Broken Function Level Authorization (CRITICAL)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API5.1 | Verify administrative endpoints accessible only to authorized administrative roles | CRITICAL | Admin endpoint access test |
| API5.2 | Confirm function-level access control enforced for all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) | CRITICAL | Method-level authorization test |
| API5.3 | Validate administrative operations require re-authentication or step-up authentication | HIGH | Step-up auth test for admin ops |
| API5.4 | Verify audit logging for all administrative actions | HIGH | Audit log review |

#### API6: Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API6.1 | Identify sensitive business flows: fund transfers, account closure, limit changes | CRITICAL | Business flow identification document |
| API6.2 | Verify additional controls for sensitive flows: additional verification, delay, cooling-off period | CRITICAL | Sensitive flow control test |
| API6.3 | Implement and verify transaction limits per user tier and verification level | CRITICAL | Limit enforcement test |
| API6.4 | Confirm high-value transaction review/approval workflow implemented | HIGH | Approval workflow test |

#### API7: Server-Side Request Forgery (HIGH)

| Item | Checklist Item | Priority | Evidence Required |
|------|---------------|----------|-------------------|
| API7.1 | Verify URL validation prevents SSRF attacks | CRITICAL | SSRF test cases |
| API7.2 | Confirm allowlist implementation for URL destinations | HIGH | URL allowlist review |
| API7.3 | Validate response body validation for fetched resources | MEDIUM | Response validation test |
| API7.4 | Implement network segmentation: API cannot access
